statement.en: Weak Convergence of Quasi-Nonexpansive Mapping by Mann's Type
statement.summary: AbstractThe present paper investigates the weak convergence behavior of iterative sequences generated by the Mann-type iteration process for quasi-nonexpansive mappings in real Banach spaces. Starting from the classical framework introduced by Mann, we extend the analysis to quasinonexpansive operators defined on closed convex subsets of Banach spaces. By employing fundamental geometric properties of Banach spaces, together with the demiclosedness principle, we establish that the operator (I −T) associated with a quasi-nonexpansive mapping T is demiclosed at zero. This property plays a crucial role in proving the weak convergence of the Mann iterative scheme. Under appropriate control conditions on the sequence{xn}, it is shown that the sequence {ρn} generated by ρn+1 = xnρn + (1 − xn)Tρn

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a mathematical theorem in functional analysis with classical precedent (Mann iteration, quasi-nonexpansive mappings, demiclosedness principle). The claimed weak convergence result under stated conditions is a natural extension of established theory (Mann 1953, Browder-Petryshyn). The proof structure (demiclosedness → weak convergence) follows standard methodology in Banach space theory. While the specific combination may be novel, the axiom is formalizable, provable-in-principle, and consistent with known fixed-point theory.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 反復は重み付き平均として、空間の幾何学に導かれ、固定点へ柔らかく収束する。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 収束する螺旋は凸空間の内部で踊り、弱い位相のもとで目に見えない固定点を目指す。
